title: DIY Hanging Planter
summary: This DIY hanging planter can be customized depending on the size you want, and it's a great addition to any space!
description: This DIY hanging planter can be customized depending on the size you want, and it's a great addition to any space!

materials.name: 1/4" thick cotton rope (cut to length of choice)

steps.title: Cut Your Wood
steps.body: Cut your wood to the dimensions listed.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Drill 2 pocket holes at one end of each of your 8" long 2x2 boards.

steps.title: Build Top and Bottom of Planter
steps.body: Attach 4 of your 8" long 2x2 pieces using 2 1/2" long pocket hole screws, forming a square. Repeat and build a second square. These will be the top and bottom of the planter.

steps.title: Drill Holes for Dowel Rod
steps.body: Choose one of your "squares" - this will be the bottom of the planter. Drill a 1/2" hole in the center of 2 of the parallel sides using a Forstner bit. This will allow you to drive a dowel rod through the holes from one side to the other.

steps.title: Insert Dowel Rod
steps.body: Use a mallet to insert your dowel rod through the holes. This will create a place for your planter to rest when everything is assembled.

steps.title: Attach Side Pieces
steps.body: Using a brad nailer and wood glue, attach your 5" long 2x2 board pieces to each corner of your squares, connecting them and creating a "box." These will form the sides.

steps.title: Stain or Paint
steps.body: Stain or paint your piece. I used a special walnut stain for this one.

steps.title: Add Screw Eyes
steps.body: Use a small drill bit to pre-drill holes in each corner of the top of your planter. Then insert screw eyes.

steps.title: Add Rope
steps.body: Cut rope to your desired length (just depends on how high you want to hang it), and thread it through the screw eyes, then tie a knot at the top.
